llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: This is a natural experiment using difference-in-differences. Python became supported by GitHub Copilot (AI assistance) in October 2021, while R remained unsupported. Python represents the treatment group (human+AI), R represents the control group (human-only baseline). Choosing the main specification (not balanced panel or SDiD) as it has the largest effect and is presented first.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a difference-in-differences natural experiment comparing Python (treated with Copilot support) vs R (control, no Copilot support). The outcome is in levels (raw commit counts), not log-transformed. The regression coefficient represents the DiD estimate (treatment × post interaction). The baseline mean of 18.395 is explicitly stated as the pre-treatment mean for the control group (R). Multiple specifications are reported (main, balanced panel, SDiD); selected the main specification as primary. The Rust vs Haskell comparison provides a robustness check but Python vs R is the main analysis.
source_quotes: For Python versus R (Columns 1-3), GitHub Copilot increases commits by 6.816 per quarter (column 1), which represents a 37.05% increase relative to the pre-treatment mean of 18.395 commits per quarter.
